page: UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a general-purpose server powered by the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 80 physical cores utilizing the AMD EPYC 7543 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, configured with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.4 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a 2048 GB SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU encryption and physics tests. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block reads reaching 5364.31 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 184.62 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation on medium 7B models is a notable bottleneck, falling into the bottom 25% with 6.53 tokens/sec.

Given its high resource density, the server offers a qualitatively balanced cost-to-performance profile for highly parallelized workloads without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration. The combination of 80 cores, 512 GB of RAM, and 2 TB of local SSD storage makes this instance well-suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput Redis caching. It is less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation due to the lack of hardware accelerators and lower single-threaded inference speeds.
description: The UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a KVM-based general-purpose server utilizing x86_64 architecture with 80 physical cores of AMD EPYC 7543 processors running at 2.0 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of system memory (6.4 GB per core) and a bundled 2048 GB SSD. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark physics and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, though single-threaded performance and LLM text generation represent relative performance bottlenecks. This server provides a resource-dense, qualitatively cost-efficient platform for highly parallelized workloads, making it suitable for large-scale software compilation, database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput Redis caching.
og_description: UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 80 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and a 2048 GB SSD. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for databases and parallel processing.
meta_description: UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a KVM server with 80 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 2 TB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and databases.
tagline: A high-core, memory-dense virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 80 AMD EPYC 7543 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz on KVM hypervisor.
512 GB of system memory paired with a bundled 2048 GB local SSD.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression speeds.
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput Redis database operations.
Best suited for software compilation, database hosting, and parallel data processing.
